tasks.title: Phase 90: Standardize transition approval rationale in Dashboard drawer
tasks.approach: Refactor transition helpers so Dashboard-originated accept, decline, and batch accept provide rationale from the drawer while command-palette flows can keep native VS Code prompts if needed.
technicalScope: Add a drawer workflow for single task transitions showing task id, action, target effect, and whether the action is policy-sensitive.
Collect non-empty policyApproval.rationale in the drawer before calling run-transition from Dashboard actions.
Add a batch accept drawer that shows task count, category, shared rationale, and failure handling for per-task run-transition calls.
Refactor confirmAndRunTransition and confirmAndRunAcceptProposedPhaseBatch or add Dashboard-specific wrappers so existing command-palette behavior is not broken.
Preserve expectedPlanningGenerationArgs, ingestPlanningMetaFromData, notifyKitStateChanged, and partial failure reporting.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard Accept/Decline row actions do not show a VS Code modal followed by a top-bar input box.
Users see the transition effect and enter policy rationale in one Dashboard drawer before mutation.
Batch accept uses one shared rationale and reports partial failures clearly inside the Dashboard or drawer result state.
A missing rationale blocks the mutation before run-transition is called.
Tests cover single-transition and batch-transition drawer state/rendering.
tasks.summary: Replace Dashboard run-transition modal + input-box sequences with a drawer confirmation that collects policy rationale in context.
tasks.risk: High: this touches policyApproval UX for task lifecycle changes and must preserve audit semantics exactly.

tasks.title: Phase 90: Align CAE Guidance prompts with dashboard input standard
tasks.approach: Treat CAE acknowledgement, feedback, and registry mutation prompts as part of the Dashboard UI plugin consistency pass, while preserving policy language and mutation gating.
technicalScope: Audit GuidanceViewProvider and GuidancePanel showInputBox/showWarningMessage usages for actor, rationale, feedback note, and registry mutation confirmation flows.
Move Guidance webview-originated acknowledgement and feedback input into the webview where it improves context and does not duplicate native VS Code command behavior.
For CAE registry mutation actions, show actor, rationale, target version, payload summary, and CAE governance approval in one in-webview confirmation flow when launched from the Guidance UI.
Keep native notifications for failures and non-webview command-palette fallbacks only where they remain the clearest VS Code behavior.
Update tests for guidance action messages or render output that changes because prompts move into the webview.
acceptanceCriteria: Guidance UI actions launched from the CAE webview no longer mix multiple native prompt styles for routine input collection.
Registry mutation confirmation still clearly says CAE mutation approval is not Tier A/B run policyApproval.
Actor/rationale requirements are validated before mutation commands run.
Existing mutation capability denial behavior remains clear and safe.
Guidance tests cover any new webview prompt/result states.
tasks.summary: Review the CAE Guidance sidebar/panel prompt surfaces and align user input with the same webview-contained drawer standard where practical.
tasks.risk: Medium-high: CAE mutation prompts carry governance copy and must not blur approval lanes.

tasks.title: Phase 90: Document and test Dashboard prompt consistency rules
tasks.approach: Codify the prompt-surface decision close to the extension code and add tests that catch accidental reintroduction of Dashboard-originated native input prompts.
technicalScope: Add or update extension README/developer guidance describing when to use the drawer, native QuickPick/InputBox, modal warnings, and notifications.
Add targeted tests for drawer rendering, form validation, busy/result states, and migrated workflows.
Add a lightweight static or unit-level guard if practical to flag new DashboardViewProvider showInputBox/showQuickPick usages for Dashboard-originated flows.
Keep tests focused on render/message behavior rather than full VS Code webview E2E unless an existing smoke path is cheap to extend.
Run cursor-workflow-cannon tests and relevant root checks for extension/dashboard changes.
acceptanceCriteria: A maintainer-facing note documents the Phase 90 prompt-surface rule and exception cases.
Tests fail if the core drawer shell or migrated workflows regress.
The extension test suite passes with Node v22.22.2 using pnpm --filter cursor-workflow-cannon test.
Any remaining native prompt use is explicitly justified as command-palette fallback, destructive modal confirmation outside the Dashboard path, or transient notification.
Release evidence for the phase can cite the prompt consistency rule and test coverage.
tasks.summary: Add regression coverage and lightweight operator/developer guidance so future Dashboard prompts follow the drawer-first UX rule.
tasks.risk: Low-medium: mostly tests/docs, but static guards must avoid noisy false positives that block legitimate VS Code command flows.

blockedBy: At least one migrated drawer flow should exist before finalizing docs and regression assertions.

tasks.title: Phase 90: Migrate Add wishlist item to drawer form
tasks.approach: Port the existing create-wishlist field collection into the shared drawer, submit one validated payload to the host, and preserve planning-generation retry behavior and wishlist detail affordances.
technicalScope: Render title, problem statement, expected outcome, impact, constraints, success signals, requestor, and evidence reference in one drawer form with required-field validation.
Move Dashboard button handling away from promptAndCreateWishlist for dashboard-originated clicks while preserving any command-palette fallback that still needs native VS Code input.
Submit create-wishlist through DashboardViewProvider using expectedPlanningGenerationArgs and the existing planning-generation refresh/retry path.
Show success, cancellation, validation, and create-wishlist failure states inline in the drawer; include an Open wishlist detail action when an id is returned.
Keep wishlist pagination reset and dashboard refresh behavior after successful creation.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Add wishlist item opens an in-dashboard drawer instead of a sequence of VS Code showInputBox prompts.
All existing wishlist fields are present, required fields are validated before submission, and empty required fields do not call create-wishlist.
Successful creation refreshes the dashboard, resets wishlist pagination to the first page, and offers a clear way to open the created wishlist detail.
Planning-generation mismatch retry behavior remains intact.
Tests cover the rendered form fields, validation messaging, and success/failure state rendering.
tasks.summary: Make Add wishlist item the proof-point migration by replacing the eight sequential VS Code input boxes with one Dashboard drawer form.
tasks.risk: Medium: changes a user-facing create flow and must preserve concurrency handling and existing task-store semantics.

tasks.title: Phase 90: Move phase assignment prompts into drawer workflows
tasks.approach: Reuse existing phase suggestions from dashboard-summary, but render them inside the Dashboard drawer with custom phase-key entry and inline validation.
technicalScope: Move assign-task-phase target selection out of showQuickPick/showInputBox and into a drawer opened from row Phase buttons.
Render collected phase suggestions with source labels such as canonical, workspace DB, config hint, catalog, and bucket labels.
Support a custom stable phaseKey entry path with validation equivalent to the current input flow.
Move Register future phase catalog entry into a drawer form with phaseKey and optional shortDescription fields.
Preserve recordActivity, expected planning generation, ingestPlanningMetaFromData, refresh, and success/error notices.
acceptanceCriteria: Task row Phase actions no longer use VS Code QuickPick/InputBox in Dashboard-originated flows.
Users can choose a suggested phase or enter a custom phase key in the drawer.
Register future phase runs upsert-phase-catalog-entry from the drawer and reports success/failure inline.
Invalid or empty phase keys are blocked before the CLI command runs.
Tests cover phase suggestion rendering and custom phase validation.
tasks.summary: Replace Dashboard phase-key QuickPick/InputBox flows with drawer-based phase assignment and phase catalog workflows.
tasks.risk: Medium: phase assignment is common queue-maintenance behavior and needs clear validation to avoid wrong phase buckets.

tasks.title: Phase 90: Convert phase note prompts to drawer flows
tasks.approach: Treat phase-note actions as small drawer workflows with clear command summaries, required rationale fields, and inline policy-sensitive confirmations.
technicalScope: Replace add-phase-note QuickPick/InputBox sequence with a drawer form for note type, summary, priority, and optional details.
Replace dismiss-phase-note reason input and critical-note policy rationale with a drawer flow that clearly distinguishes audit reason from policyApproval rationale.
Move convert-phase-note-to-task and propose-tasks-from-phase-notes persist confirmations into drawer confirmations with command and effect summaries.
Keep critical dismiss policy behavior intact, including policyApproval when required by workspace config.
Render command errors inline and refresh phase notes after successful mutations.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard phase-note actions do not open top-bar input boxes for normal Dashboard-originated use.
Add note validates type, summary, and priority before calling add-phase-note.
Critical dismiss clearly requires and submits policyApproval rationale when applicable.
Convert and persist actions show the command effect before execution and support cancel without mutation.
Tests cover add-note form rendering, dismiss validation, and confirmation copy for convert/persist.
tasks.summary: Bring phase note add, dismiss, convert, and persist interactions into the Dashboard drawer so note workflows share one prompt pattern.
tasks.risk: Medium-high: phase notes can carry policy-sensitive state, especially critical dismissals; copy must avoid implying chat-only approval satisfies policy.

tasks.title: Phase 90: Dashboard input drawer contract
tasks.approach: Introduce a shared Dashboard input-drawer contract and host/webview message protocol before migrating individual flows. Keep workspace-kit CLI commands as the only mutation path; the drawer only collects and validates user intent.
technicalScope: Add a reusable Dashboard drawer shell in the dashboard webview with title, description, form body, validation area, primary/secondary actions, busy state, and result status.
Define a typed message protocol between render-dashboard and DashboardViewProvider for opening, submitting, cancelling, and reporting drawer workflows.
Support select, text input, textarea, confirmation, and read-only summary field types needed by existing prompt flows.
Handle focus management, Escape/cancel behavior, keyboard submit where appropriate, and responsive layout inside the VS Code sidebar webview.
Keep existing planning interview behavior working while allowing it to remain inline or later move to the shared drawer without breaking saved planning state.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard-originated prompts have one documented default: use the in-webview drawer for user input and wizard steps.
The drawer can render at least one multi-field form and one confirmation+rationale flow without calling showInputBox or showQuickPick from the Dashboard path.
Drawer cancel, validation failure, command failure, and command success are visible in the Dashboard without losing the user's place.
No webview code mutates SQLite or kit-owned state directly; all mutations still go through CommandClient workspace-kit commands.
Unit tests cover the drawer shell rendering and at least one message-to-state transition.
tasks.summary: Standardize Dashboard-originated user prompts and wizards on a reusable drawer overlay inside the webview so users are not bounced between the Dashboard, top command input, and modal popups.
tasks.risk: Medium: introduces shared UI state in a frequently used sidebar; must avoid breaking existing dashboard refresh and details-open preservation.

decisionRecord.promptSurface: Reusable drawer overlay inside the webview

blockers: Confirm drawer overlay design remains the Phase 90 UX direction.

data.listTasksCursorSemantics: Keyset pagination: opaque cursor from prior nextCursor; under concurrent updates rows may move across pages (re-run without cursor for a fresh ordering snapshot).

cae.agentRenderHint: If present, summarize CAE shadow outcome in one short paragraph; when traceId is set, operators can run cae-explain / cae-get-trace.
